messages.text:
HAWAIIAN VOLCANO OBSERVATORY STATUS REPORT
U.S. Geological Survey
Wednesday, July 15, 2026, 6:08 PM HST (Thursday, July 16, 2026, 04:08 UTC)
KILAUEA VOLCANO(VNUM #332010)
19°25'16" N 155°17'13" W,
Summit Elevation 4091 ft (1247 m)
Current Volcano Alert Level: ADVISORY
Current Aviation Color Code: YELLOW
Summary: Episode 51 of the ongoing Halemaʻumaʻu eruption began at 8:30 a.m. HST and ended at 4:46 p.m. HST on July 15, 2026.
Activity Summary: Episode 51 of the ongoing Halemaʻumaʻu eruption began around 8:30 a.m. HST on July 15 and ended abruptly at 4:46 p.m. HST on July 15, 2026.The maximum lava fountain height was approximately 950 feet (290 meters) above ground level from the north vent. The south vent never fountained during this episode but did emit flames and gas jets.The maximum plume height was approximately 18,000 (5,500 meters) above mean sea level.Tephra fall was restricted mostly to the closed area of Hawaiʻi Volcanoes National Park to the southwest of the active Halemaʻumaʻu vents. A light fall of Peleʻs hair was reported at several sites in the national park outside the closed area, including Uēkahuna overlook and Kaʻu Desert trailhead on Highway 11.Episode 51 Chronology:Lava fountaining episode 51 in Halemaʻumaʻu at the summit of Kīlauea began around 8:30 a.m. HST on July 15 and ended abruptly at 4:46 p.m. HST, after 8.3 hours of continuous fountaining from the north vent. The last 2-3 minutes were marked by gas jetting from the north vent. The south vent never fountained during this episode but did emit flames and gas jets. The instantaneous effusion rate peaked at about 370 cubic yards (280 cubic meters) per second at approximately 10:30 a.m. HST, with an average effusion rate of 200 cubic yards (150 cubic meters) per second for the entire fountaining episode. An estimated 6.6 million cubic yards (5.1 million cubic meters) of lava erupted and covered about 50% of the Halemaʻumaʻu crater floor. Seismicity followed the normal change in tremor patterns seen after most fountain episodes. There was one locatable earthquake associated with the eruption: a shallow magnitude-2 event at 14:52:59 HST located beneath the southeast corner of Halema'uma'u crater. The Uēkahuna tiltmeter (UWD) recorded about 14.7 microradians of deflationary tilt during episode 51.Precursory activity began from the north vent on the afternoon of July 14 with 4 overflows between 3 and 4 p.m. HST, the longest lasting over 30 minutes. messages.subject: Jay, join us for Building the AI-Native Startup Stack
messages.snippet: Date & time: Fri, Aug 14, 1:00 PM (PDT) Address: Canopy - 1300 El Camino Real Ste 100, Menlo Park CA 94025 Agenda: RSVP About this event​What You'll Experience​🎙️ Keynotes & Panels​Hear from the technology providers who powered the AI prod
messages.text:
Date & time: Fri, Aug 14, 1:00 PM (PDT)
Address: Canopy - 1300 El Camino Real Ste 100, Menlo Park CA 94025
Agenda:
RSVP
About this event​What You'll Experience​🎙️ Keynotes & Panels​Hear from the technology providers who powered the AI products at massive scale, covering voice agents, generative media, and the emerging agent stack. No pitch decks. Real talk and practical insights from the teams driving AI innovation.​🕹️ Four Hands-On Demo Stations​Communication AI — Build your own voice agent live across the full stack: RTC, ASR, TTS, LLM, and open ecosystem integrations - 130+ languages, less than 300ms latency. Come with your use case and leave with a blueprint.​Media & Creator AI — AI-native multimedia capabilities that power the full content workflow, from generative creation to intelligent editing, media enhancement, processing, and distribution. Built for live streaming, AI filming, short-form content, e-commerce, online education, and creative production teams.​Web App & Agent AI — Deploy full-stack web apps and AI agents globally in minutes, powered by Tencent Cloud's EdgeOne Makers — the edge platform that hit #1 on Product Hunt for both Product of the Day (June 24, 2026) and Product of the Week. Serverless and zero-DevOps, with AI-assisted development via MCP. Designed for lean startup teams who need to validate fast and scale instantly.​TokenHub — Explore Tencent Cloud's unified LLM gateway for accessing leading foundation models through a single API. Featuring industry-standard protocol compatibility, automatic fallback, and cost-aware routing with built-in TPM governance. Simplifies model management and reduces inference costs while building AI applications at scale.​🎟️ Free Trial Credits​Walk away with hands-on trial access to every product showcased. No credit card required.​🤝 Curated Networking​Structured networking hours with food & drinks. The room will be 150–200 hand-picked AI founders, engineers, media builders, researchers, and AI-focused VCs. ​Who Should Attend​AI startup founders and engineers building voice, media, or agent products​Media and entertainment teams working on streaming, content creation, or asset management​Engineering leads managing inference costs and token economics at scale​Early-stage builders looking for zero-overhead deployment platforms​AI-focused VCs and researchers looking for the full-stack view​This is an approval-only event. Registrations are reviewed to ensure a high-signal room. Apply below.
Stay eng

messages.subject: Silicon Valley Doesn't Do Summer - Startup Grind July Update
messages.snippet: Silicon Valley Doesn't Do Summer - Startup Grind July Update Hi all,Silicon Valley doesn't slow down for summer. If anything, this is when the best founders do their sharpest work while everyone else is on the beach. This month's round
messages.text:
Silicon Valley Doesn't Do Summer - Startup Grind July Update
Hi all,Silicon Valley doesn't slow down for summer. If anything, this is when the best founders do their sharpest work while everyone else is on the beach. This month's roundup has something for founders at every stage: an almost sold-out flagship event, a format change worth showing up for, an AI agent that can incorporate your company in one prompt, and a new international program looking for mentors.Female Founder Series, 5th Edition, Shack15, SFWe're close to sold out again. We've moved to a bigger room at Shack15 just to keep up with demand. Top-tier VC speakers, and the most promising female founders in software, AI, and hardware across the Bay Area.We're also expanding to New York City this September, partnering with Inc. Magazine.→ Grab one of the final tickets AI Demo Series — July 28, New FormatJuly's edition goes open mic. Half the presenters are drawn at random from who's checked in, so if you're in the room, you've got a real shot at the stage. Show up in person, not just on the list.→ RSVP for a chance to presentRamp for Agents: Idea to Incorporated Company in One PromptStartup Grind runs on Ramp, and we're fans. But this isn't just for us. It's for founders at any stage, including pre-launch. Ramp's new AI agent takes you from "I have an idea" to an operating company: US incorporation and financial ops set up, in a single prompt. If you're bootstrapped or pre-seed and dreading the admin, this removes it.Startup Grind members get access to:• $500 free just for signing up• $350,000 in rewards for tools like AWS, OpenAI, Notion...• Uncapped cash back, ~4% on reserve cash, 27% average SaaS savings• No-fee travel booking + 3× faster month-end closesFounders building from zero are also using Ramp for Agents to incorporate their company and set up finances in one go.→ Start with Ramp for AgentsNEW PROGRAM: J-StarX AI GTM Support Course: Startup Grind x JETROWe just launched a 6-month program in partnership with JETRO, built to help Japan's best AI founders land in the US market.Japan has some of the sharpest AI builders anywhere and this cohort is coming to learn what actually works here: GTM, fundraising, positioning, distribution. If you've done the US market entry yourself, or just know the ecosystem, this is a great way to plug in and meet founders early.This cohort is pre-seed to Series A, and the focus is hands-on: customer validation, identifying ICPs and prospects, and t

messages.subject: ChatGPT shamelessly copied Claude.
messages.snippet: View this post on the web at https://ruben.substack.com/p/chatgpt-copied-claude ChatGPT finally copied Claude: They announced: A new mega-app for ChatGPT. Codex, their coding tool, is now inside ChatGPT. A new mode inside the ChatGPT app, c
messages.text:
View this post on the web at https://ruben.substack.com/p/chatgpt-copied-claude
ChatGPT finally copied Claude:
They announced:
A new mega-app for ChatGPT.
Codex, their coding tool, is now inside ChatGPT.
A new mode inside the ChatGPT app, called ChatGPT Work.
The new flagship model (5.6) with three levels of intelligence: Sol (very smart), Terra, and Luna (very cheap).
This entire newsletter will answer one question:
Does ChatGPT still matter?
Yes, it matters.
OpenAI is one of the two leading AI labs in the world.
ChatGPT will always matter, with its 5 billion monthly visits:
But does it matter enough to switch from Claude?
Harder question. Let’s find out.
1. How to access the new ChatGPT.
You need to:
Uninstall the old ChatGPT app (if you had it before).
Download it (again, I know) at chatgpt.com/download [ https://substack.com/redirect/678d62fd-7778-471a-94bc-5899e18b1472?j=eyJ1IjoiN2EzYzFhIn0.gmSjAtdlofMqOFUjgfQgnPyMBlCD_j00mTQEinip6mM ].
You must have a paid account (at least $20/month).
It will look like this once you open it:
Now that you have access to the new ChatGPT, it’s time for you to know the truth.
2. It’s so confusing, I’m lost.
These are the 30 combinations of the new ChatGPT.
ChatGPT Work? Codex? Which model? Which effort? Good luck.
Let me answer as simply as possible.
The first distinction is Work vs. Codex:
ChatGPT Work is to work on anything but code. Like Claude Cowork, it spawns many ChatGPT (agents) to answer your queries.
ChatGPT Codex is for developers. It’s the same, but with a design and an interface made for developers.
So you want to stay on ChatGPT Work.
Now, the second one is the different models:
You have 5.5 (old one) and 5.6 (the new one).
5.6 comes in 3 flavors of intelligence…
Luna: cheap but dumb.
Terra: the cost/smartness balance.
Sol: the super smart super expensive one.
If that was it, that would be so easy… but no… you also have different efforts.
It’s quite obvious that the more effort, the smarter the model, but the more expensive it is. What’s not obvious at all is which one is better between
5.6 Sol (the smartest one) on Light effort?
or 5.6 Luna (the dumbest one) on Extra High effort?
and also, WHY DO WE HAVE TO PICK??? I can’t believe it.
So I tried to make a graph to explain it better… but it’s also a bit confusing. I’m sorry. I don’t work at OpenAI or Anthropic. I also wish it were easier.
I’ll save you some thinking time:
The best expensive one: Sol-Ultra, only for planning and a couple of turns.
The one y

messages.subject: Tomorrow: Cohort 10 Info Session — July 15, 9 AM PT
messages.snippet: **Over 1,300 people** have already registered to attend our live** Info Session for Cohort 10 — tomorrow **(Wednesday, July 15) **at 9:00 AM **(PT). Applications are open to Cohort 10 of the [Climatebase Fellowship](https://climatebase.org/
messages.text:
**Over 1,300 people** have already registered to attend our live** Info Session for Cohort 10 — tomorrow **(Wednesday, July 15) **at 9:00 AM **(PT).
Applications are open to Cohort 10 of the [Climatebase Fellowship](https://climatebase.org/fellowship?utm_source=weekly_newsletter&utm_medium=newsletter&utm_campaign=cbf10&utm_content=cbf10-7126-isb), and like every cohort, spots are already filling up.
If you’re interested in learning more, attending the Info Session is your best opportunity to do so. [RSVP here.](https://luma.com/iug6lflp?utm_source=navbar)
Here's what we're covering tomorrow during the Fellowship Info Session:
* What the twelve weeks actually look like — the curriculum, expert guests, career support, Capstone projects, and the community
* What to expect in Cohort 10 (running September 8 – December 4, 2026)
* **NEW: A sneak peak at our 2026 Career Outcomes Report **— an analysis of how the Fellowship helped accelerate the careers of over 1,000 Fellows from our first six cohorts.
* Who should apply, how to apply, and what financial aid is available
* How year-round community membership keeps delivering long after the program ends
* Answers to your questions, through live Q&A
You’ll also hear real stories from past cohorts — the roles Fellows landed, the projects and ventures they launched, and the community they built along the way.
**RSVP for the Info Session →** (https://luma.com/iug6lflp?utm_source=navbar)
### **Can’t attend live?**
Comment “Climatebase" on [Evan’s LinkedIn post](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/evanhynes_over-1250-people-have-rsvpd-for-tomorrows-share-7482919159958224896-s7Rj/) and **we’ll send you the recording** of the Info Session. 👇
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/95ee0c9b-2c2d-4f13-8769-ac8f76221f0d/Screenshot_2026-07-14_at_3.17.32_PM.png?t=1784067599)
Follow image link: (https://www.linkedin.com/posts/evanhynes_over-1250-people-have-rsvpd-for-tomorrows-share-7482919159958224896-s7Rj/)
Caption:
**Apply to Cohort 10 →** (https://climatebase.org/fellowship?utm_source=weekly_newsletter&utm_medium=newsletter&utm_campaign=cbf10&utm_content=cbf10-7126-isb)
———————————————————————————
# **The rest of this email covers:**
* Key dates for Cohort 10
* Testimonials and recap from Cohort 9
* Fellowship overview: Who joins and why? – Plus curriculum, guest speakers, capstone projects & more
* Success stories, and what employers say about

messages.snippet: Quick Tips #3 of 3 https://inaturalist.org/ Hi jkhasty, In 2024, a Swedish tourist in New Zealand spotted a moth — and accidentally rediscovered a species scientists had considered extinct for over 50 years. The Frosted Phoenix has since be
messages.text:
Quick Tips #3 of 3
https://inaturalist.org/
Hi jkhasty,
In 2024, a Swedish tourist in New Zealand spotted a moth — and accidentally rediscovered a species scientists had considered extinct for over 50 years. The Frosted Phoenix has since been documented on iNaturalist almost 20 times.
https://www.ted.com/talks/scott_loarie_the_surprising_power_of_your_nature_photos
Frosted Phoenix seen by pav_johnsson in New Zealand
Stories like this happen all the time on iNaturalist:
Discovering new-to-science species
https://www.inaturalist.org/pages/newsletter
A woman walking her neighborhood in Australia photographed a mantis, and it turned out to be a species new to science, now named Inimia nat.
Tracking invasive species
https://www.inaturalist.org/pages/newsletter
A London commuter noticed a tiny insect and shared it on iNaturalist — an invasive bug not recorded in the UK for 18 years.
Protecting local ecosystems
https://www.inaturalist.org/pages/newsletter
Local volunteers in California used iNaturalist to coordinate protection for amphibians migrating across a busy road.
This is community science: millions of people noticing, documenting, and protecting the nature around them. Every observation you share becomes part of the global scientific record — and helps train the identification tool that makes iNaturalist useful for everyone.
Want more stories like these? Sign up for The Nature Notice, our free monthly newsletter
Sign up for The Nature Notice https://www.inaturalist.org/pages/newsletter
Have fun exploring with iNaturalist!
With gratitude,
​— The iNaturalist Team
https://inaturalist.org/
Our mission is to connect people to nature and advance biodiversity science and conservation.
iNaturalist is free, nonprofit, and community-powered — by people like you.
As a reminder, you created your iNaturalist account jkhasty using julia.hasty@gmail.com. If you are having trouble logging in, you can regain access by entering julia.hasty@gmail.com
on the password reset page https://www.inaturalist.org/users/password/new.
For more support: Visit our Getting Started https://help.inaturalist.org/en/support/solutions/folders/151000552105 page or reach out to help@inaturalist.org mailto:help@inaturalist.org with your questions.
iNaturalist Team
PO Box 150357, San Rafael, CA 94915

Happy Canada Day (July 1st 🇨🇦) and Happy July 4th 🇺🇸! This month I spent July 1st, 🇨🇦 Canada Day at Joanne Fedeyko Pathway to Silicon Valley's reception that helps Canadian female founders enter the US market (top right). messages.snippet: View this post on the web at https://bayareafoundersclub.substack.com/p/last-call-claude-code-masterclass ⏰ [ https://substack.com/redirect/20fd944f-77f5-4ff7-a44d-728d71ae5f50?j=eyJ1IjoiN2EzYzFhIn0.gmSjAtdlofMqOFUjgfQgnPyMBlCD_j00mTQEinip6
messages.text:
View this post on the web at https://bayareafoundersclub.substack.com/p/last-call-claude-code-masterclass
⏰  [ https://substack.com/redirect/20fd944f-77f5-4ff7-a44d-728d71ae5f50?j=eyJ1IjoiN2EzYzFhIn0.gmSjAtdlofMqOFUjgfQgnPyMBlCD_j00mTQEinip6mM ]Last Call: Claude Code Masterclass Starts Tomorrow [ https://substack.com/redirect/20fd944f-77f5-4ff7-a44d-728d71ae5f50?j=eyJ1IjoiN2EzYzFhIn0.gmSjAtdlofMqOFUjgfQgnPyMBlCD_j00mTQEinip6mM ]
Most people use AI one conversation at a time.
The next generation of AI-native professionals is building systems instead.
Instead of asking AI to summarize documents, prepare reports, or organize research over and over again, they’re building reusable workflows that automate those tasks and save hours every week.
That’s exactly what you’ll learn in our next BFC AI Academy workshop.
Claude Code Masterclass — Building a WhatsApp Bot from Scratchwith a Stanford Instructor [ https://substack.com/redirect/20fd944f-77f5-4ff7-a44d-728d71ae5f50?j=eyJ1IjoiN2EzYzFhIn0.gmSjAtdlofMqOFUjgfQgnPyMBlCD_j00mTQEinip6mM ]
This is a hands-on, 4-session workshop for founders, investors, operators, advisors, and AI builders who want to move beyond prompting and start building real AI-powered workflows around their work.
You’ll learn how to:
✅ Build a WhatsApp AI bot from scratch using Claude Code
✅ Create reusable AI workflows that automate repetitive work
✅ Connect AI with real-world tools and APIs
✅ Develop practical AI systems you can use in your business immediately
Whether you’re a technical builder or a non-technical professional, this workshop will help you start thinking like an AI systems designer—not just an AI user.
🎟️ We’re now less than 24 hours away, and this is your last chance to join.
💻 Live on Zoom
⏰ 4-Session Hands-on Workshop Series
👥 Founders, Investors, Operators, Advisors & AI Leaders
If you’ve been waiting to start building with AI instead of just chatting with it, this is the workshop for you.
See you tomorrow! 🚀

messages.snippet: Startup Bulletin NLR Innovation and Entrepreneurship Center Hello Innovators, Hope you are having a fabulous summer! The July edition of the bulletin includes new opportunities for your company, along with an important transition announceme
messages.text:
Startup Bulletin
NLR Innovation and Entrepreneurship Center
Hello Innovators,
Hope you are having a fabulous summer!
The July edition of the bulletin includes new opportunities for your company, along with an important transition announcement from the NLR Innovation and Entrepreneurship Center.
After nearly six years serving as Startup Engagement Lead, I will be stepping down from this role and passing the baton to my colleague, Andy Goeke. It has been a privilege to work with such a passionate and dedicated community of innovators developing new technologies. Thank you for your collaboration and engagement over the years.
Andy will continue compiling funding opportunities and distributing this monthly bulletin. I’ll still be at NLR and look forward to seeing many of you at the next IGF.
In this issue:
* Learn about our new data center test bed, Agora
* A reminder of our upcoming webinar on the partnering with the lab
* An ARPA‑E Notice of Funding Opportunity (NOFO)
* Events organized by our partners
* Additional funding opportunities from our partners
Don’t forget to visit the Startup Resource Center for the latest NLR publications (https://hissing-weeder-b4d.notion.site/Notable-Publications-f2bfd4545489459cbd96f13aa120230e) , events (https://hissing-weeder-b4d.notion.site/Events-2c7c02e57b4d806687f1c76c25e2464d) , and other helpful information.
Best,
Monali Mujumdar (monali.mujumdar@nlr.gov (mailto:monali.mujumdar@nrel.gov) ) and Andy Goeke (andy.goeke@nlr.gov (mailto:andy.goeke@nlr.gov) )
NLR Innovation and Entrepreneurship Center
Startup Resource Center (https://hissing-weeder-b4d.notion.site/Resources-157fb5317c7f429fb76f19aec433cfa5)
NLR Spotlight - New Data Center Test Bed Unveiled
The U.S. Department of Energy’s National Laboratory of the Rockies (NLR) today unveiled the Agora large-load test bed, a first-of-its-kind national capability designed to help data centers become active participants in grid reliability.
Agora is funded by the U.S. Department of Energy’s Office of Electricity and industry partners and was designed in close collaboration with industry and utilities to address real-world challenges. It is the only dedicated large-load grid integration test bed across the U.S. national laboratory complex and replicates the technical complexity of a large-scale data center interconnection.
Read more about the test bed at its capabilities in the article: NLR Launches Agora, First-of-Its-Kind Large-Load Grid Integration Test Bed (https://www.nlr

messages.subject: AEG 26Q3 Results: Affordability & Grid Optimization
messages.snippet: AEG 26Q3 Results: Affordability & Grid Optimization Held on July 9th, 2026 at Pepco Edison Place Gallery in Washington, DC, seventy public and private industry leaders convened for the AEG DMV 26Q3 Affordability & Grid Optimization Stakehol
messages.text:
AEG 26Q3 Results: Affordability & Grid Optimization
Held on July 9th, 2026 at Pepco Edison Place Gallery in Washington, DC, seventy public and private industry leaders convened for the AEG DMV 26Q3 Affordability & Grid Optimization Stakeholder Challenge. The purpose of this challenge was to: 1.) Align on a critical obstacle regarding Affordability & Grid Optimization for the DMV; 2.) Engage as cross sector teams to agree on a 90-day sprint and 12-month goal; 3.) Empower leaders to deliver the solution and present the outcome in 12 months with 90 day sprints. Tyler Anthony, President & CEO, Pepco, Ted Trabue, Commissioner, DC Public Service Commission, and Kumar Barve, Chairman, Maryland Public Service Commission, opened the conversation, providing diverse perspectives to frame the discussion surrounding affordability & grid optimization for DMV. Josephus Allmond, Chief Energy Officer, Office of the Governor of Virginia served as a pre-challenge moderator, posing and answering audience questions to create a road map for the upcoming workshop. During the Speaker Challenge, each speaker provided a presentation that concluded with this completed statement: "Regarding Affordability & Grid Optimization, to achieve DMV’s health, prosperity & energy goals, a critical obstacle to collectively overcome in 12 months is _________." 5 Key Themes 1. Developing a Distribution Grid Utilization Roadmap To achieve meaningful rate relief, the region must shift from a default infrastructure buildout to a flexibility focus that unlocks unused capacity on the existing electric system. Currently, the grid is designed around rare peak demand hours, leaving less than half of its asset capacity used on average. By aligning utilities, regulators, and data center operators on a unified utilization roadmap, grid costs can be shared across a larger customer base to lower average retail rates. 2. Siting and Funding Distributed Energy Storage Deploying localized energy storage provides an immediate shield against soaring wholesale capacity prices while traditional generation projects face multi-year construction timelines. This immediate infrastructure deployment is vital in Virginia, which has overtaken California as the nation's leading net importer of generation. Failing to fund and site these battery projects increases regional vulnerability to rolling brownouts, climate-related health risks, and escalating bills. 3. Dismantling Energy Efficiency Gaps for Small Businesses Cost-burden
